acceptance_criteria: No persisted artifact, served dashboard response, jj status output, or jj runs output contains raw API keys, common token patterns, private key blocks, configured secret values, raw command text, raw environment values, unsafe absolute paths, omission placeholders, raw artifact bodies, raw manifests, raw diff bodies, validation output payloads, planner handoff payloads, raw TASK.md bodies, raw EVAL.md bodies, raw document bodies, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.
Traversal attempts using relative, absolute, encoded, or symlinked paths cannot read or write outside the configured workspace or allowed run artifact roots.

Dry-run and non-dry-run produce equivalent redaction, boundary, diagnostic, validation output, planner handoff metadata, git diff redaction behavior, artifact persistence, and safe path label persistence.

Existing TASK.md summary, latest-run summary, next-action summary, Project Docs shortcuts, Recent Runs, Evaluation Findings, Active Run, Validation Status dashboard behavior, run detail Validation Evidence, Compare Previous, Run Artifacts behavior, jj status behavior, and jj runs behavior remain unchanged when sanitized presentation helper maintenance is performed.

jj status and jj runs exit successfully in a valid workspace, use only shared sanitized DTO/helper output, and render deterministic none, unavailable, unknown, or denied states for malformed, denied, stale, hostile-label, token-like, partial, tied timestamp, missing timestamp, or internally inconsistent metadata without panics or unsafe reflection.

Focused CLI and serve tests cover the sanitized status, recent-runs, dashboard root, TASK summary, run detail, validation evidence, compare-previous, run artifact states, Project Docs, Evaluation Findings, Latest Run action-link, Active Run, and Validation Status dashboard behavior states touched by each task.

Completed security guardrails remain closed after TASK-0096 because focused Validation Evidence serve tests, go test ./internal/serve, go test ./internal/cli ./internal/serve, go test ./..., git diff --check, and scripts/validate.sh passed and no current validation, test, disclosure, or boundary evidence shows a concrete regression.

Dashboard Latest Run summary and action-link rendering remain byte-for-byte or behaviorally identical for valid, empty, unavailable, denied, malformed, partial, stale, hostile-label, token-like, missing timestamp, tied timestamp, and internally inconsistent metadata states touched by the refactor.
Latest Run guarded URLs, action ordering, labels, visibility, timestamps, status/evaluation text, fallback messages, and spacing are preserved.

Latest Run helper logic is narrow, unexported, and consumes only existing sanitized DTO/helper output, guarded URL decisions, fixed safe labels, timestamps, counts, status/evaluation values already normalized by existing code, or already-safe state metadata.

Latest Run refactoring introduces no raw manifest, artifact body, raw path, command text, environment value, diff body, validation payload, planner handoff payload, TASK.md body, EVAL.md body, document body, configured secret, token-like value, omission placeholder, unsafe file read, route, CLI flag, schema field, config behavior, scanner, upload, raw export, visual redesign, redaction policy change, path-boundary policy change, or user-facing semantic change.

Focused serve coverage is preserved or strengthened for Latest Run summary, guarded action-link, no-link, denied, unavailable, unknown, none, malformed, partial, stale, hostile-label, token-like, missing timestamp, tied timestamp, internally inconsistent, and fallback behavior.

TASK-0097 centralized dashboard Latest Run summary, primary line, secondary provider/result line, guarded action-link, ordering, timestamp/status/evaluation text, and fallback presentation construction through narrow unexported internal/serve helper logic only where behavior is identical.

TASK-0097 consumes only existing sanitized Latest Run DTO/helper output, guarded URL decisions, fixed safe labels, timestamps, status/evaluation values already normalized by existing code, or already-safe state metadata.

TASK-0097 preserved dashboard Latest Run labels, action ordering, guarded URLs, visibility, fallback messages, spacing, timestamps, denied, unavailable, unknown, none, malformed, partial, stale, hostile-label, token-like, tied timestamp, missing timestamp, internally inconsistent, no-link, and fallback behavior.

TASK-0097 introduced no raw manifest, artifact body, raw path, command text, environment value, diff body, validation payload, planner handoff payload, TASK.md body, EVAL.md body, document body, configured secret, token-like value, omission placeholder, route, CLI flag, schema field, config behavior, scanner, upload, raw export, visual redesign, redaction policy, path-boundary policy, or user-facing semantic change.

TASK-0097 preserved or strengthened focused serve coverage for exact rendered Latest Run rows, helper output, guarded action-link availability, no-link behavior, fallback behavior, denied, unavailable, unknown, none, malformed, partial, stale, hostile-label, token-like, tied timestamp, missing timestamp, and internally inconsistent metadata.
TASK-0097 validation passed focused Latest Run serve tests, go test ./internal/serve, go test ./internal/cli ./internal/serve, go test ./..., git diff --check, and scripts/validate.sh.

Completed security guardrails remain closed after TASK-0097 because focused Latest Run serve tests, go test ./internal/serve, go test ./internal/cli ./internal/serve, go test ./..., git diff --check, and scripts/validate.sh passed and no current validation, test, disclosure, or boundary evidence shows a concrete regression.
goals: Generate concrete specs and tasks from a single plan file while preserving an auditable run history.

Keep generated artifacts, served files, plan inputs, workspace mutations, validation outputs, git diff artifacts, planner and Codex handoff metadata, dashboard summaries, active-run progress, validation status, run-detail validation evidence, compare-to-previous links, run artifact inventory, CLI status output, CLI recent-run output, and run inspection surfaces inside the resolved workspace or guarded run artifact roots.

Prevent secrets, token-like values, private key material, omission placeholders, raw commands, raw environment values, unsafe paths, raw manifests, raw artifact bodies, raw diff bodies, validation output payloads, planner handoff payloads, raw TASK.md bodies, raw EVAL.md bodies, raw document bodies, and attacker-controlled denied payloads from being persisted or rendered.

Provide a dashboard-first local jj serve experience with guarded run detail, history, comparison, sanitized audit export views, compact sanitized TASK.md work-queue summary, latest-run summary, next-action summary, Project Docs shortcuts, Recent Runs, Evaluation Findings, Active Run progress, Validation Status, Validation Evidence, Compare Previous, and Run Artifacts.
Provide compact sanitized CLI summaries for quick workspace pre-flight and run-history checks without starting jj serve.
Preserve dry-run and non-dry-run workflows with identical redaction, path-boundary, diagnostics, artifact persistence, diff redaction, validation, and planner handoff metadata behavior.
Ensure auto and balanced task proposal resolution selects bugfix or security only from concrete current evidence, not completed guardrails, healthy context, or absent evidence.
Use scripts/validate.sh as the single documented release validation entry point for local and CI security release gates.
Keep completed security guardrails closed unless a concrete release-gate, CI, validation, test, disclosure, or boundary regression is observed.

Keep sanitized CLI, dashboard root, run-detail, TASK summary, next-action, Project Docs, Recent Runs, Evaluation Findings, Active Run, Validation Status, Latest Run action-link, Validation Evidence, Compare Previous, Run Artifacts, and run-summary presentation code maintainable by reducing unnecessary duplication, nesting, and branching without changing output semantics.
non_goals: Provide a multi-user dashboard or authentication system.
Replace git review or guarantee AI output correctness.

Expose raw commands, environment dumps, secret values, sensitive absolute paths, private key material, token-like strings, omission placeholders, raw artifact bodies, raw manifests, raw diff bodies, validation output payloads, planner handoff payloads, raw prompt exports, raw TASK.md bodies, raw EVAL.md bodies, raw document bodies, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.
Add raw manifest export, raw artifact export, raw diff export, raw prompt export, artifact content bundling, raw artifact upload, raw manifest upload, or raw diff upload.

Add new dashboard pages, CLI flags, raw export endpoints, broad file import behavior, scanners, pagination, configurable dashboard limits, configurable recent-run limits, configurable active-run limits, validation execution controls, .jjrc project defaults, or unrelated user-facing behavior as part of sanitized presentation helper maintenance.
Allow explicitly provided plan files outside the workspace by default.
Reopen completed tasks without fresh failing validation, tests, CI, confirmed disclosure, or boundary regression evidence.
Expose child command stdout or stderr from the release validation entry point or CI wrapper.

Change jj status, jj runs, dashboard layout, manifest schema, artifact routes, redaction policy, path-boundary policy, validation behavior, or user-facing output semantics as part of sanitized presentation helper maintenance.
requirements: jj run must read a non-empty Markdown plan from the explicitly provided path using documented path resolution behavior, then require the resolved plan file to remain inside the configured workspace boundary after canonicalization and symlink evaluation.

Rejected external, traversal, absolute-escape, encoded-escape, symlink-escape, unreadable, malformed, or secret-looking plan inputs must produce deterministic sanitized errors before content read or artifact persistence.

Accepted plan content, planner output, Codex output, command summaries, evaluation output, validation output, docs writes, manifest serialization, event writes, run artifact writes, git diff artifacts, dashboard DTOs, CLI status DTOs, CLI recent-run DTOs, and rendered dashboard or terminal data must pass through shared redaction before persistence or display.

Manifest and general JSON persistence must use safe workspace, run, relative, or generic path labels and must not store raw workspace, plan, external config, git, repository, validation command, or baseline paths.
Command execution records must persist sanitized argv, cwd labels, status, duration, and result metadata only; raw command text and raw environment values must not be stored or rendered.

Validation stdout and stderr artifacts and git diff artifacts must be sanitized before persistence; evidence reports may include only booleans, counts, sanitized category labels, and safe relative artifact labels.

Dry-run and non-dry-run modes must apply identical plan path validation, redaction, path-boundary, artifact persistence, git diff capture, diff redaction, manifest metadata, diagnostics, validation output sanitization, planner handoff sanitization, and denial behavior.

jj serve must only serve files from guarded workspace and run artifact roots and must reject traversal, absolute, encoded, secret-looking, token-like, duplicate hostile query, or symlink escape inputs with deterministic denied or unavailable states.
Run IDs may contain only safe letters, numbers, dots, underscores, and dashes, must not use traversal-like forms, and must be rejected when they match configured secrets or common token patterns.

Run detail, history, comparison, audit export, dashboard TASK summary, dashboard latest-run, next-action, Project Docs, Recent Runs, Evaluation Findings, Active Run, Validation Status, Validation Evidence, Compare Previous, Run Artifacts, jj status, and jj runs must obtain data through shared sanitized DTO construction or shared helpers, not local raw manifest, artifact, command, environment, diff, validation output, EVAL.md body, document body, or filesystem reads after route or CLI input parsing.

Untrusted, stale, partial, malformed, manually edited, or internally inconsistent manifest fields, artifact references, validation summaries, command metadata, diff metadata, provider fields, timestamps, evaluation metadata, findings, active-run metadata, validation-status metadata, validation-evidence metadata, compare-previous metadata, run-artifact metadata, status metadata, recent-run metadata, TASK-summary metadata, latest-run metadata, and next-action hints must render only deterministic denied, unknown, unavailable, all-clear, none, or sanitized states.

The dashboard root must preserve existing sanitized TASK.md summary, latest-run summary, next-action summary, fixed Project Docs shortcuts, Recent Runs, Evaluation Findings, Active Run, and Validation Status behavior.
Existing guarded run detail views must preserve compact Validation Evidence, Compare Previous, and Run Artifacts behavior using only shared sanitized DTO/helper output and guarded URL decisions.

jj status and jj runs must exit successfully in a valid workspace and print compact sanitized summaries using only shared sanitized DTO/helper output and deterministic none, unavailable, unknown, or denied states.

Sanitized presentation helpers may consolidate common safe run ID, status, evaluation, validation, timestamp, detail URL, audit URL, CLI run-summary line, ordered CLI run-summary fields, numbered label selection, dashboard guarded action-link construction, Latest Run action-link construction, Latest Run action ordering selection, Recent Runs item construction, Recent Runs fallback construction, TASK summary construction, next-action construction, Project Docs shortcut construction, Validation Evidence summary construction, Validation Evidence item construction, Validation Evidence guarded action construction, Validation Evidence fallback construction, Compare Previous summary and guarded-link construction, Run Artifacts item construction, Run Artifacts fallback construction, Run Artifacts guarded action construction, Evaluation Findings summary construction, Active Run item construction, and Validation Status item construction only when they accept existing sanitized DTO/helper output, guarded URL decisions, fixed expected categories, route or file state metadata where applicable, or already-safe labels and preserve existing behavior.

Dashboard Latest Run presentation construction may be simplified through narrow unexported helpers that consume only existing sanitized DTO/helper output, guarded URL decisions, fixed safe labels, timestamps, counts, status/evaluation values already normalized by existing code, or already-safe state metadata.

Dashboard Latest Run simplification must preserve labels, action ordering, visibility, spacing, timestamps, status/evaluation text, guarded URLs, fallback messages, denied, unavailable, unknown, none, malformed, partial, stale, hostile-label, token-like, missing timestamp, tied timestamp, internally inconsistent, no-link, and fallback behavior.
Guarded presentation refactors must consume only existing sanitized DTO/helper output, guarded URL decisions, fixed safe labels, or already-safe labels.

Guarded presentation refactors must preserve dashboard-root and run-detail labels, ordering, visibility, guarded links, denied states, unavailable states, unknown states, none states, empty states, hostile-label handling, token-like handling, malformed or partial metadata handling, spacing, fallback messages, deterministic display data, and surrounding semantics for every touched path.

Guarded presentation refactors must not introduce raw manifest, artifact body, raw path, command text, environment value, diff body, validation payload, planner handoff payload, TASK.md body, EVAL.md body, document body, configured secret, token-like value, omission placeholder, arbitrary file read, unsafe path read, route, CLI flag, schema field, config behavior, pagination, scanner, upload, broad file browsing, raw export, visual redesign, redaction policy change, path-boundary policy change, or user-facing semantic change.

Auto or balanced task proposal resolution must choose bugfix only from positive current blocker evidence and security only from concrete release-gate, CI, validation, test, disclosure, or boundary evidence showing a security or privacy regression.

scripts/validate.sh must remain the single documented release validation entry point, run focused security boundary suites, full tests, vet, CLI build, and diff-check, suppress child command output, emit only deterministic sanitized metadata, and exit non-zero on any failing step.

Completed security guardrails must remain closed unless scripts/validate.sh, focused Go security suites, full tests, vet, build, diff-check, or the CI release gate produces a concrete disclosure, path-boundary, artifact, dashboard, planner handoff, validation output, git diff, command metadata, or release-gate output regression.
summary: jj is a CLI that turns a planning file into implementation-ready specs, tasks, Codex runs, validation artifacts, and a local dashboard. Security remains the controlling constraint: generated artifacts, served run-inspection surfaces, explicit plan inputs, command metadata, manifests, validation outputs, git diff artifacts, planner and Codex handoff metadata, run metadata, dashboard summaries, project document shortcuts, recent runs, evaluation findings, active-run progress, validation status, run-detail validation evidence, compare-to-previous links, sanitized run artifact inventory, CLI status output, and CLI recent-run output must be guarded, redacted, and kept inside the configured workspace or allowed run artifact roots. Completed security guardrails remain closed unless concrete validation, test, CI, disclosure, or boundary evidence proves a regression. Sanitized presentation code may continue to be simplified through narrow behavior-preserving helpers. TASK-0090 through TASK-0097 completed narrow sanitized presentation refactors for TASK summary, Recent Runs, Evaluation Findings, Run Artifacts, Compare Previous, Active Run, Validation Status, run-detail Validation Evidence, and dashboard Latest Run presentation construction while preserving behavior, routes, schemas, redaction, path-boundary behavior, and user-facing semantics.
title: jj Security Boundary and Dashboard Run Inspection
